title: Abort when using a tuple subclass instance as the `bases` parameter for `type`
author: devdanzin
body:
# Crash report

### What happened?

The interpreter will abort when a new type is created using an instance of a subclass of `tuple` as the `bases` parameter due to a `PyTuple_CheckExact(bases)` assertion:
```python
class weird_tuple(tuple): pass

c = type("c", weird_tuple((str,)), {})
python: Objects/typeobject.c:500: set_tp_bases: Assertion `PyTuple_CheckExact(bases)' failed.
Aborted (core dumped)
```

Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.

### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

### Output from running 'python -VV' on the command line:

Python 3.14.0a6+ (heads/main:04bc681e7cf, Apr  4 2025, 12:38:19) [GCC 11.4.0]

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-132212
* gh-132548
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: xuantengh
comments.body: The [doc](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type) states the 2nd arg when `type()` receives 3 arguments should be a `tuple`. Maybe we should raise an exception if `bases` cannot pass the `PyTuple_CheckExact` check?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
> Maybe we should raise an exception if `bases` cannot pass the `PyTuple_CheckExact` check?

Maybe, but it works in release builds, resulting in a class where `__bases__` is the instance of the tuple subclass we passed to `type`. So there's a chance somebody out there is using this in some way.

comments.author: xuantengh
comments.body: Otherwise we should relax the assert with `PyTuple_Check`.

comments.author: sobolevn
comments.body:
I think that it should be an exact tuple for `_Py_SetImmortal` call and it can be a tuple subclass for regular classes. This problem exists since 3.12

Thanks for the report! 🤝
